Empires & Puzzles Guillemette Review: The Sanguine Crusader's Bloody Value

Is Guillemette the holy savior your roster needs or just another average speed hero? We dive into her unique Blood Lily mechanics, her anti-mana-control utility, and why she might just be the Rush War queen you've been looking for.
Guillemette Review: Holy AOE & The Blood Lily Curse
Guillemette: Because "Holy" and "Blood Magic" are Apparently Friends Now
Guillemette, the "Sanguine Crusader," arrives from the

family, bringing a gothic, vampiric vibe to the usually bright and cheery Holy element. Released in October 2025, she represents a shift towards "slow burn" mechanics with her family's signature Blood Lily stacks. She's a 5-star Legendary Paladin who specializes in hitting everyone, protecting her team's mana, and then hitting people again when they aren't looking. If you've ever wanted a hero who punishes enemies just for existing with high HP, she's your gal.

Visually, Guillemette is striking. She sports a pair of impressive, curved horns that would make a demon jealous, paired with pale skin and dark, ornate armor that screams "I'm not here to heal you." It's a stark contrast to the traditional golden-armored paladins of the Holy element. She looks like she walked out of a heavy metal album cover directly into your puzzle board. The "Blood Lily" theme is subtle but present in the red accents, reminding us that this crusader is definitely tapping into some darker arts.
When we look at Guillemette's stats (1328 Attack / 1215 Defense / 2249 Health), she sits in an interesting spot.
Her 1328 Attack is quite robust, making her hits sting.

At 1215, she is noticeably softer than the absolute latest heavyweights (like the Magic Tower heroes pushing 1300+ Defense). As a Paladin, she can mitigate this with her class talent, but she is squishier than you might expect for a tank-style class.
2249 is a solid, modern health pool, keeping her alive long enough to fire.
Compared to the bleeding edge of the meta (January 2026), she is slightly behind on the defensive front but remains competitively aggressive.
"Unholy Power" is a multi-layered skill that rewards patience and synergy.
is a decent opener. It's not the hardest hit in the game (compared to Slow heroes), but for Average speed, it softens the entire board effectively.

for 4 turns is the hidden gem here. This shuts down some of the most annoying heroes in the game—think Alfrike, Waterpipe, or any mana-cutters. It ensures your team keeps charging.
The secondary effect deals 135% damage to anyone marked with a
at the end of the turn. This turns her into a damage-over-time engine. If you have the family synergy going, this damage adds up fast, effectively giving her a second hit every turn for 4 turns.

If an enemy has a Blood Lily, they take an additional 135% damage per turn. Over 4 turns, that's potentially
Her passive adds +5% damage per Blood Lily stack. If a target has 5 stacks (rare but possible), her base hit climbs to ~295%.
The mana immunity doesn't have a damage number, but in Rush wars, it effectively prevents infinite damage from the opponent by stopping their mana control.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Fleur de Sang family is all about the
. It's a parasitic mechanic that punishes the healthiest enemy.

At the start of every turn, the enemy with the highest HP gets a Blood Lily. This stack deals damage (DOT) and heals the Fleur de Sang family members. It's a "rich get poorer" mechanic. It ensures there is almost always a target for Guillemette's secondary damage effect.

is a straightforward damage booster. The more Blood Lilies on the target, the harder she hits. It encourages bringing other Fleur de Sang heroes to ramp up stacks quickly, but even alone, the family bonus guarantees at least one target is taking extra pain.
for the first 6 turns is top-tier. It guarantees she won't be silenced, blinded, or defense-downed before she can fire her first volley. This makes her incredibly reliable on defense or offense.
Released in late 2025, Guillemette entered a meta crowded with powerful AOE hitters. She carved a niche not by pure damage, but by utility.
While her defense stat (1215) is starting to look a bit 2024 compared to the 1300+ monsters of 2026, her skills are timeless. Mana immunity is a mechanic that never goes out of style.

wars and tournaments where her Average speed becomes Very Fast, and her mana immunity blocks the format's biggest threats. In standard raids, she's a solid B+ hero—good, but needs the right team to truly dominate.
Guillemette is a Paladin, which gives her natural bulk, but her role is dealing damage.

The family bonus heals her even in Bloody Battle? (Check mechanics: usually steal/gain health works, but standard regen doesn't. Since it says "gain... as health", it might be blocked, but the damage remains). The pure damage output is high.
Mana immunity protects against curses or floor effects that cut mana.
Layering protections. Guillemette stops mana cuts, Poseidon stops mana prevents/silence, Aramis provides ailment immunity. You become unstoppable.
Stacking Fleur de Sang heroes ramps up Blood Lily stacks insanely fast. This maximizes Guillemette's passive damage and the secondary 135% hit, while Ludovico heals off the massive DOT.
(Ailment Blockers) prevent the Blood Lily mark? No, the mark is usually uncleansable or a stack, but check if it can be blocked. If it's a stack, it typically bypasses standard immunity.

Be careful pairing her with other "Immunity" buffers to ensure you don't waste turns.
. A 5-star hit-all hero is always useful for map stages and farming. Her built-in healing (via family bonus) makes her sustainable for hard map levels where you don't have a dedicated healer.

. If you already have a deep roster, she might not crack your main raid team, but she is a luxury pick for specific wars. Do not prioritize her for Alpha Aethers unless you really need a Holy option for Rush Defense.
"I am looking forward to the passive that just kills one random enemy hero at the start of the battle."
- A forum user joking about the power of the Blood Lily stacks.
"This is really forcing players to either dig deep for a complete set or skip entirely."
- Community sentiment on the family bonus synergy requirement.
Many players initially underestimated the value of the
, focusing too much on the damage numbers. It turns out, not losing your mana is actually quite good for winning.

Guillemette is a hero who bridges the gap between brute force and tactical utility. She isn't the loudest nuke in the room, but she is the one ensuring everyone else gets to fire their nukes by protecting their mana. While her stats aren't cutting edge anymore, her toolkit remains sharp.
A solid addition to any roster, specifically for those who hate having their mana stolen.

A detailed review of Guillemette, the 5-star Holy Paladin from the Fleur de Sang family in Empires & Puzzles. We analyze her stats, 'Unholy Power' special skill, and Blood Lily mechanics to see if she's worth your ascension materials.
